skill.name: agentdb-performance-optimization
skill.description: Optimize AgentDB vector database performance with quantization, HNSW indexing, caching, batch operations, and monitoring guidance.
skill.summary: Tune AgentDB for faster vector search, lower memory use, and better scaling.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command and network patterns, but semantic review found they are documentation examples in SKILL.md. The real concern is the recommendation to run npx agentdb@latest, which executes remote package code and creates supply-chain risk. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Remote Package Execution Recommended
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run npx agentdb@latest for benchmarks and statistics. This is legitimate for AgentDB tooling, but @latest can execute newly published remote package code without pinning or verification.
medium_findings.recommendation: Warn users to verify the package source, pin trusted versions, and run commands in a controlled environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and would execute a package from npm. The context is legitimate benchmarking, so this is a supply-chain risk rather than confirmed malicious activity.

low_findings.title: SQLite Database Path References Are Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged .db paths as sensitive files. The referenced paths are example AgentDB database locations in configuration and monitoring snippets, not evidence of secret access or exfiltration.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep database paths generic in examples and remind users not to expose production database files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines show sample dbPath values and stats output fields. I found no file-reading logic, credential handling, or network transfer of database contents.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs appear in a Learn More section. They are references to the project repository and website, not executable network requests.
low_findings.recommendation: No change is required for static links, but marketplace review should verify destination trust before publishing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links are plain markdown references with no surrounding code that fetches data. This strongly supports a documentation-only interpretation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe performance optimization and memory reduction. They do not define or invoke cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss this static alert unless future versions add actual cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found only descriptive prose about AgentDB performance. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic API usage appears there.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned npx Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The pattern npx agentdb@latest can install and execute the latest package version at runtime. This is useful for a quick start but has dependency trust risk.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Prefer pinned package versions and verify package ownership before running the command.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact command appears in bash examples. The risk depends on npm package trust, not on malicious code embedded in this skill.

content.value_statement: Large vector stores can become slow and memory heavy. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through AgentDB tuning options for faster search and smaller storage.
actual_capabilities: Explains binary, scalar, product, and full precision quantization tradeoffs.
Provides HNSW parameter guidance for speed, recall, and memory use.
Shows cache sizing strategies for small, medium, and large AgentDB workloads.
Documents batch insert and parallel retrieval patterns for vector workflows.
Includes monitoring commands and metrics for AgentDB performance checks.
Offers tuning recipes for speed, accuracy, memory limits, and scale.
limitations: It is guidance documentation, not an automated optimizer.
Benchmark claims depend on hardware, data shape, and AgentDB version.
Some examples require installing and trusting external AgentDB packages.
It does not validate production data quality or embedding model behavior.

use_cases.title: Reduce Memory Costs
use_cases.description: Choose quantization settings that reduce vector storage while preserving acceptable search accuracy.

use_cases.title: Improve Search Latency
use_cases.description: Tune HNSW and cache settings for faster semantic search in AgentDB-backed applications.

use_cases.title: Plan Vector Scale
use_cases.description: Select recipes for workloads from small prototypes to million-vector deployments.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Quantization Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose an AgentDB quantization mode for my vector database. My dataset has [count] vectors and I need [accuracy or memory goal].

prompt_templates.title: Tune Search Latency
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my AgentDB search latency goal of [target latency]. Recommend HNSW and cache settings for [vector count] vectors.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Scaling Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an AgentDB scaling plan for [application type]. Include quantization, caching, batch operations, monitoring metrics, and expected tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Performance Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this AgentDB performance configuration for risk, latency, memory use, and accuracy tradeoffs. Suggest safer pinned commands where external tools are used.

output_examples.input: I have 500,000 vectors and memory pressure is high.
output_examples.output: Recommended path: scalar quantization first, binary only if memory remains constrained. Increase cache gradually and measure recall after each change.

output_examples.input: Search latency is above 20 milliseconds for a large AgentDB index.
output_examples.output: Recommended checks: confirm HNSW indexing, lower efSearch only after recall testing, increase cache size, and compare warm versus cold queries.

output_examples.input: We need a production benchmark checklist before launch.
output_examples.output: Checklist: pin AgentDB package versions, record hardware, test representative embeddings, capture cache hit rate, measure recall, and document memory savings.

best_practices: Pin external package versions before running benchmark or stats commands.
Measure recall and latency together when changing quantization settings.
Start with balanced settings, then tune one parameter at a time.
anti_patterns: Do not use binary quantization without validating accuracy loss.
Do not trust @latest package commands in production automation.
Do not compare benchmark results without matching hardware and data shape.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my AgentDB database automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides configuration examples, commands, and tuning guidance for a user to apply.

faq.question: Which quantization option should I start with?
faq.answer: Start with scalar quantization when you need a balanced mix of memory savings and accuracy.

faq.question: Why is npx agentdb@latest a security concern?
faq.answer: It can execute the newest remote package version. Pin a trusted version before using it in repeatable workflows.

faq.question: Can this help with million-vector datasets?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes large-scale recipes using quantization, HNSW tuning, and larger cache sizes.

faq.question: Does it guarantee the listed benchmark improvements?
faq.answer: No. Actual improvements depend on hardware, embeddings, data distribution, and AgentDB version.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a warning about external commands. No malicious intent or prompt injection was found.
